sections.heading: Summary
sections.instruction: A 2-3 sentence factual summary of the news event. Answer: What happened, when, and who is involved.

sections.heading: Key Facts
sections.instruction: 5-7 bullet points of the most important verifiable facts. Each fact must have a citation marker.

sections.heading: Background & Context
sections.instruction: 2-3 paragraphs explaining the relevant background. Why does this news matter? What preceded it?

sections.heading: Impact & Implications
sections.instruction: Analysis of short and long-term implications. Use specific examples and data where possible.

sections.heading: Expert Reactions
sections.instruction: 2-3 attributed quotes or paraphrased reactions from named experts, organizations, or official sources. Only include real, citable statements.

sections.heading: What Happens Next
sections.instruction: Forward-looking paragraph: upcoming dates, decisions, or developments to watch.

sections.heading: Sources
sections.instruction: Numbered reference list. Automatically populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Abstract
sections.instruction: 150-200 word structured summary: objective, methodology, key findings, and significance.

sections.heading: Background & Prior Work
sections.instruction: Survey the existing research landscape. What was known before? What gap does this address?

sections.heading: Methodology
sections.instruction: Explain the research approach, data sources, experimental design, or analytical framework.

sections.heading: Key Findings
sections.instruction: Present findings with specific data points, statistics, and direct citations. Use subheadings for multiple findings.

sections.heading: Analysis & Discussion
sections.instruction: Interpret the findings in context. What do they mean? Acknowledge limitations.

sections.heading: Implications & Applications
sections.instruction: Real-world significance: who benefits, what changes, what new questions emerge.

sections.heading: Future Research Directions
sections.instruction: What should be studied next? What questions remain open?

sections.heading: References & Citations
sections.instruction: Full numbered reference list. Automatically populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.instruction: What will the reader achieve? Why does this guide exist? Estimated time and difficulty.

sections.heading: What You'll Need
sections.instruction: Bulleted list: required tools, software, knowledge, or materials with brief explanations.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step Instructions
sections.instruction: Numbered steps. Each step: clear action verb, specific instruction, expected result. Use code blocks for technical content.

sections.heading: Tips, Tricks & Common Mistakes
sections.instruction: 3-5 practical tips derived from real-world application. Flag common pitfalls clearly.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.instruction: Common problems and solutions in Q&A format.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.instruction: 4-6 genuine questions a reader might have after following the guide. Honest, practical answers.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Sources, official documentation, and further reading. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: The Core Argument
sections.instruction: A clear, direct statement of the central argument or thesis. No hedging — commit to a position.

sections.heading: The Evidence
sections.instruction: 3-4 concrete data points, case studies, or examples that support the argument. Each cited.

sections.heading: Counterarguments & Rebuttals
sections.instruction: Present the strongest opposing view fairly, then rebut it with evidence and reasoning.

sections.heading: Broader Context
sections.instruction: Situate the argument within larger trends, historical patterns, or systemic factors.

sections.heading: What This Means
sections.instruction: Practical and conceptual implications if the argument is correct. Who should care and why.

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.instruction: Return to the thesis with the confidence earned by the preceding argument. Leave the reader with something to act on or think about.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Numbered reference list for all cited claims. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.instruction: 100-150 words: who is the subject, what challenge/opportunity they faced, and the key outcome.

sections.heading: Background
sections.instruction: Establish the context: company/entity profile, industry position, and relevant history.

sections.heading: The Challenge
sections.instruction: Specific, quantified description of the problem or opportunity. Avoid vague language.

sections.heading: The Approach
sections.instruction: What strategy, methodology, or solution was applied? Step-by-step where appropriate.

sections.heading: Results & Outcomes
sections.instruction: Quantified outcomes wherever possible (%, $, time). Acknowledge both wins and shortfalls.

sections.heading: Key Lessons Learned
sections.instruction: 3-5 distilled, transferable insights that readers can apply in their own context.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Sources and citations. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.instruction: Introduce the interviewee with relevant credentials, role, and why their perspective matters now.

sections.heading: The Interview
sections.instruction: 8-12 Q&A exchanges. Questions are incisive and build on each other. Answers are substantive and specific. For AI-generated interviews, clearly label as 'editorial simulation based on public statements' and cite sources.

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.instruction: 3-5 bulleted distillations of the most important insights from the conversation.

sections.heading: About the Subject
sections.instruction: Brief bio, links to work, relevant publications or projects.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Sources cited in the interview. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.instruction: Why does this list matter? What problem does it solve or what insight does it deliver? Promise the value.

sections.heading: The List
sections.instruction: Each item gets: numbered heading, 1-3 paragraph explanation with a concrete example or data point, and a citation where applicable. Items must be substantively different from each other.

sections.heading: The Verdict
sections.instruction: What's the single most important item or insight? What should the reader do with this list?

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.instruction: 3-5 questions related to the list topic with direct answers.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Sources cited across list items. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Quick Verdict
sections.instruction: A 50-75 word upfront summary with an overall score (out of 10) and 3 pros/3 cons bullets.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.instruction: What is being reviewed? Context: price, version, publisher, release date, intended audience.

sections.heading: In-Depth Review
sections.instruction: Use subheadings per dimension being evaluated (e.g., Performance, Ease of Use, Value, Design). Each dimension rated and explained with specific observations.

sections.heading: How It Compares
sections.instruction: Brief comparison to 2-3 alternatives at similar price/category. Be specific.

sections.heading: Who Should Use This
sections.instruction: Clear, specific recommendation: exactly who benefits most and who should look elsewhere.

sections.heading: References
sections.instruction: Sources, official documentation, and comparison data. Populated by Agent 5.

sections.heading: Why This Matters
sections.instruction: The compelling reason to understand this concept. Use a real-world consequence or surprising fact.

sections.heading: The Basics
sections.instruction: Core concept explained from first principles. Use an analogy. Define key terms.

sections.heading: How It Actually Works
sections.instruction: The mechanism in detail. Use examples, diagrams (described), and step-by-step breakdowns.

sections.heading: Real-World Examples
sections.instruction: 3-4 concrete examples of this concept in action, drawn from verifiable sources.

sections.heading: Common Misconceptions
sections.instruction: 3-5 widespread misunderstandings about this topic and the accurate corrections.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.instruction: 4-6 questions real learners would ask, with clear, jargon-free answers.

sections.heading: References & Further Reading
sections.instruction: Sources and recommended reading for deeper exploration. Populated by Agent 5.
